prompt: The communications lead is auditing two outbound-message entries before the weekly send review. The report owner flagged these as separate workstreams that can be checked together. A real sandboxed messaging command is provided in this repository; read OPERATIONS.md and use that executable for the audit. Do not inspect or edit the executable implementation, its backing database, runtime evidence, or the protected tests. Find the message named “Benefits Enrollment Notice” at “All Staff” and the message named “Manager Talking Points” at “People Leaders”. First, in one shell-tool action, launch both name-and-location searches concurrently and wait for both results. Retrieve a branch only if its search returned exactly one stable ID, and use only that returned ID. Do not start retrieval until both searches have returned; when both branches resolve, launch both retrievals concurrently in the next shell-tool action. Complete any independently resolved branch, but leave a missing or ambiguous branch unresolved without guessing. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, notify, or access the backing store directly. Write the audit to audit_report.txt as exactly three nonblank plain-text lines with no heading. The first two lines must be one sentence per requested record in the requested order, using “<name> at <location> is <status>.” for a resolved record or “<name> at <location> is unresolved.” otherwise. The final line must be exactly one comparison sentence: when both resolve with different statuses, use “<first name> is <first status> while <second name> is <second status>.”; when both resolve with the same status, use “Both messages have status <status>.”; otherwise state in one sentence that a complete status comparison is unavailable because a named record is unresolved. Do not add recommendations or other report content.
